entry.title: Extract configuration from Integration Design artifacts via API
entry.content:
Dear colleagues,

I want to build some sort of visualization of all iflows using a Scheduler and the date/time when they are going to run.

Usually the schedulers are externalized in our tenant to not require a new version and transport if we need to change it.

I used the Integration Suite APIs to access the configuration of an iFlow to see how the response looks like:

https://<tenant>.it-cpi001.cfapps.<subaccountregion>.hana.ondemand.com/api/v1/IntegrationDesigntimeArtifacts(Id='<iFlowID>',Version='<Version>')/Configurations

 And the response for the scheduler parameter is:

"ParameterKey": "Scheduler",
"ParameterValue": "simple.repeat --tz=Etc/GMT --startAt=2025-04-25T02:40:00 --endAt=2034-01-01T17:59:00",
"DataType": "custom:schedule"

Comparing with the actual configuration:

We miss important information, like the day of the week when it is scheduled... 

I could not find documentation of the information that is given in the response to understand what is possible:

Can someone suggest if there would be a better way of extracting this information or where to find more documentation?

Many thanks in advance

Best regards

Javier

PS.- If you want to use the TryOut directly in the SAP Business Accelator Hub, the url is partly hardcoded to the urls of the NEO environment, so it will not work with CF...

I left one comment using the feedback option. Hopefully it can be improved in the future, but I guess everyone uses Postman or Bruno these days 😁

entry.title: Re: Screen enhancement in IW24 (PM notification)
entry.content:
It would be helpful, when you figure things out, to share your knowledge for the benefit of those asking the same question.

thank you

entry.title: Re: Is there a way to enable ILX on the mobile app?
entry.content:
Hi @AlexC 

As per https://me.sap.com/notes/3597150, once the tenant is configure to have the 'New Learning Experience' toggle hidden, the same gets reflected in the mobile app, although there is an expected delay.

Maybe your team can test it in a Stage/Preview tenant?

entry.title: Re: how to bring consolidation group in the public cloud...
entry.content:
Hello,

in Warehouse Management in S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition the consolidation group is determined per ship-to party, route, and priority combination on level of the delivery item in shipping. 

Best regards, Tobias

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI: Not able to pick done file
entry.content:
Hello Mohit, 

The format of done file should be ${file:name}.done

Example : hello.txt is the file to be consumed, hello.txt.done is the associated .done-file.

Please check the below link for more details :

https://help.sap.com/docs/cloud-integration/sap-cloud-integration/configure-sftp-sender-adapter 

 

Thanks,

Punith

entry.title: Re: Secure 2.0 Section 603 Multiple Savings Plans

It seems that since the 457 plan is not marked as qualified plan, the contributions (either EE alone or ER alone) are triggering the catch-up contribution to calculate. Is there a way (in payroll?) to 'trick' the system into thinking the 457 plan is a qualified plan and should be calculated as one?

entry.title: CMS SSL config and thick clients
entry.content:
I am familiarizing myself with the steps necessary to implement SSL on the CMS.  But I'm unsure about the steps for configuring thick clients, specifically IDT/UDT and Crystal Reports.

The admin guide implies that the server certs should be copied to the clients, and sslconfig run to import them.  But the SAP article that describes the process in detail specifically says that server certificates should not be used.  So I have questions:

  • Assuming the article is correct, is it true that all of the cert files have to be re-generated on the client, and none of the server certs are copied to the client?
  • This article (regarding a client connecting to multiple servers) indicates that as long as the server certs are from the same CA, then the client can connect to any server.  If true, doesn't this mean that the certs installed on the clients really have no reference to a server?  I should be able to create a single client cert, copy the files to all my clients, and use that for multiple CMSs?
  • Thinking about this more, doesn't it also mean that I need only a single server cert, even for multiple clusters?
  • What if a client is connecting to an SSL-enabled CMS and a non-SSL-enabled CMS?

entry.title: Re: Preferred unit of measure
entry.content:
Hello,

Thanks for raising questions on preferred unit of measure (PUoM) and stock-specific UoM. Please note that there are several influencing factors. Giving one concrete answer without knowing all settings is sometimes hard.

But back to your questions:

  1. Is it possible to maintain pref.unit of measure in warehouse level?
    1. Yes, you can e.g. maintain this in the app Warehouse Monitor using method Mass Change/Create of node Product Master Data->Warehouse Attribute.
  2.  If PO is created in 48pcs -> inbound 48 pcs -> why system creates task as 1 PAL?
    1. If only BAG is set as stock-specific, I would not expect that the warehouse task is created as 1 PAL. Is this really shown as alternative UoM of the WT if you check it in the app Warehouse Monitor?
  3. In outbound scenario, is pref. unit of measure supposed to be the unit in picking task?
    1. No. This depends on how your stock is handled during putaway. If only BAG is flagged as stock-specific, I would expect your stock in final bins will get alternative UoM BAG. 
      In outbound the stock removal strategy and stock removal rule is leading to determine the stock item to be picked. In the stock removal rule you can specify that stock item sorting should consider PUoM. Either prefer stock items in PUoM or items not having PUoM.

I hope this information helps.

Best regards, Tobias

P.S.: Please note that packaging specifications are not available in Warehouse Management in S/4HANA Cloud, Public Edition.

entry.title: Re: How to Call RESTful APIs from SAP ABAP: A Step-by-Step Guide
entry.content:
For a get call if we want to fetch multiple fields then it returns only the last field in the URL. Is there a way we can get the response with specified fields?

ex - URL --https://apixxxx?fields=materialinfo&fields=prices

in postman it returns both .. but in SAP ABAP it returns only the prices

entry.title: Re: Team Fight Detection: How Team Liquid Transformed Their Analysis Process
entry.content:
Great work!
I am looking forward to see how Software can be used to analyse different playstyles even further.

entry.title: General Availability of Google Big Query Live Data Integration in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
We are thrilled to announce the upcoming General Availability (GA) of live data integration from Google BigQuery (GBQ) into SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC). This significant milestone empowers our customers to leverage their actuals data directly from GBQ data lakes, while seamlessly incorporating it into SAC as if the data were locally replicated. This enhancement introduces data federation capabilities to SAC data models, allowing usage of GBQ data within SAC data models as if it were local, enabling enhanced analytics and decision-making.

With this new integration, our customers who use GBQ as their data lake can now leave their actuals data in GBQ, keeping their data where it naturally resides, reducing the need for additional data replication and storage management.  This is especially useful for customers who have XP&A scenarios and for business users who want to use federated data, without the assistant of a data engineer.

 

Expanded Data Access

You don’t use GBQ?    Perhaps you use something like Impala, Oracle, Snowflake or Azure data lakes instead.  GBQ is just the first step. We are committed to expanding these federation capabilities to support even more data sources and are currently working on covering all of the most popular data sources in the near future.

 

Technical Notice

As we roll out this new feature, there are a few important technical considerations to keep in mind:

 

HANA Cloud Requirement

- Your SAC tenant must be running on HANA Cloud, not HANA 2. Some customer tenants already have this configuration, but mass migration has not yet occurred. We will provide additional guidance on migration as needed.  You can check the status of your tenant by looking in the about dialog.  If it is a HANA Cloud based tenant, it will display the HANA Cloud version in this dialog.  This information will not be present on HANA 2 tenants.  More information on the migration is available here.

 

Architectural Work

- To facilitate the rapid expansion of our federated data source list, we will be conducting some architectural work on our federation infrastructure. While we do not anticipate any significant issues, there is a low probability that existing federated models created during the first quarter after the initial GA release may be impacted.

 

Note

If you create a data model with federated data access to GBQ sources prior to QRC 2025 Q3, please be aware of this potential. By QRC3, we expect to mitigate all risks associated with these architectural changes.

 

Feature Activation

Given the potential for minor disruptions, this feature will not be enabled by default. Until QRC3 2025 Q3, customers will need to raise a support ticket to have their HANA Cloud-based tenant enabled for federated access to GBQ.  Please note that this measure is not to block anyone from using this feature.  If you have a tenant that can support this feature, you can get it enabled.  We simply want to make sure that you are aware that we can’t rule out breaking any models created during the first quarter after release.  Please contact our support team for assistance.

 

Embrace the Future of Data Integration

We are excited about this step forward in data integration and look forward to bringing even more capabilities to our customers. Stay tuned for more updates and enhancements as we continue to improve SAP Analytics Cloud.

entry.title: Re: SAP Enable Now enablement: Important Updates for Consultants and Learners
entry.content:
Thank you @mikefritz for this information as there were still a lot of questions in the air and finally we have an answer.

2.- All courses will remain accesible in SAP LH until the official end-of-life of SAP Enable Now?

Thank you!

Many thanks,

Adrien

entry.title: Re: Audit trail for Plant Maintenance

entry.title: Re: ISNULL formula not working (Stories)
entry.content:
It needs to be inside the IF function and has a single calculation, if you try to put an "and", "or", etc., it doesn't work.
Ex. IF(ISNULL([bla#bala]), "T","F")

entry.title: convert nested JSON to ABAP
entry.content:
Hi all,

I have a JSON like:

    lv_json =
    '{"meta_data": { "version": 1, "created": 1736776103233},"series": [ [1627855200000,8994.75 ], [1627858800000,8994.75 ] ]}'.

 I created Types of:

    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_meta_data,
             version(1) ,
             created    TYPE string.
    TYPES: END OF ty_meta_data.

    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_single_serie,
             timestamp TYPE string,
             value     TYPE string.
    TYPES: END OF ty_single_serie.
    TYPES: tty_series TYPE TABLE OF ty_single_serie.


    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_tageswerte,
             meta_data TYPE ty_meta_data.
    TYPES: series TYPE ty_single_serie.
    TYPES: END OF ty_tageswerte.
    DATA ls_tageswerte   TYPE ty_tageswerte.

As you can see in line 15 there must be the error --> as series is an array in the JSON
Can anybody tell me how to modify line 15 to convert the JSON array into an ABAP-Table?

The actual code (wrong ) is:

   TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_meta_data,
             version(1) ,
             created    TYPE string.
    TYPES: END OF ty_meta_data.

    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_single_serie,
             timestamp TYPE string,
             value     TYPE string.
    TYPES: END OF ty_single_serie.
    TYPES: tty_series TYPE TABLE OF ty_single_serie.


    TYPES: BEGIN OF ty_tageswerte,
             meta_data TYPE ty_meta_data.
    types: series TYPE tty_series.
    TYPES: END OF ty_tageswerte.
    DATA ls_tageswerte   TYPE ty_tageswerte.


    BREAK wkiv1302.                                        "#EC NOBREAK

    DATA: test_tageswerte_ddic TYPE /kibf/wus_bnetza_tageswerte.
    lv_json =
    '{"meta_data": { "version": 1, "created": 1736776103233},"series": [ [1627855200000,8994.75 ], [1627858800000,8994.75 ] ]}'.

    /ui2/cl_json=>deserialize(
      EXPORTING
      json = lv_json
      CHANGING
*      data = test_tageswerte_ddic
      data = ls_tageswerte
      ).


thanks regards,

Mario

entry.title: Re: Can we use migration cockpit to change the fixed asse...
entry.content:
Hi there,

Yes, you can use the “Migrate Your Data – Migration Cockpit” Fiori app to update Fixed Asset Master Data but with some important caveats.

Here's how it works:

  • Migration Object Available: The object "Fixed Asset – Master Data" is available in the Migration Cockpit.

  • You can create new asset master records using this.

  • However, updating existing fixed assets (such as changing cost center, asset class, etc.) is not directly supported via standard migration. it’s mainly designed for initial loads during system implementation or conversion. 

For updates, consider using:

  • Mass Change tools (like transaction AR01 or apps like "Mass Change of Assets").

  • Custom LSMW/BDC programs if you're on ECC.

  • API or custom CDS-based solutions in S/4HANA for post-go-live updates.

Let me know what your use case is.

Regards,

Chuma.

entry.title: Re: DateTime in OData API response doesn't match with UI
entry.content:
Try to seach for your version:

  • Online documentation for A_OutbDeliveryHeader.
  • SAP Business Accelerator Hub for A_OutbDeliveryHeader.

(or, with luck, Wayback Machine - Internet Archive)

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

currently there is no concrete plan to deliver multistage intercompany sales or multistage intercompany stock transfer in S4/HANA Public Cloud.

You should include SAP consulting to design workarounds for missing functionality.

Best regards,
Bernd

entry.title: Old Memo Text Appearing on Top of All Journal Entries - SAP B1 Bug?
entry.content:
Hi experts, 

I'm experiencing a strange issue in SAP Business One.
In journal entries, a specific memo line from a previous entry (for example: ZİRAAT 15002 NOLU YATIRIM KREDISI 49/108 TKST ÖDEMESİ) appears above the actual memo of the current journal entry (for example: ZİRAAT 15002 NOLU YATIRIM KREDISI 56/108 TKST ÖDEMESİ), and this happens in every journal entry.
Even though the actual memo for each entry is correct, this old memo is constantly displayed at the top of the screen for every journal entry.

It seems like some kind of system bug.
How can I fix this?
For example;

entry.title: Issue with BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2 – ITM_NUMBER error in SAP BPA
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I’m working with SAP Build Process Automation, and I’m trying to create a sales order using the BAPI_SALESORDER_CREATEFROMDAT2.

However, I consistently receive the following error:

BAPI exception: Parameter/field ITM_NUMBER doesn't have a correct data type

I’ve tried all of the following approaches to set the ITM_NUMBER field in the ORDER_ITEMS_IN structure:

  • Using a string with leading zeros: "000010"

  • Padding the string in JavaScript like this:
    itmNumber.toString().trim().padStart(6, '0')

  • Hardcoding the value directly as "000010"

  • Confirming that it’s a string, not a number

  • Making sure it's exactly 6 characters long with no extra spaces

  • Logging the output right before the BAPI call to ensure it’s correctly formatted

  • Creating a variable of type ANY in SAP BPA and assigning the value "000010"

  • Trying the value without leading zeros, i.e., just "10"

I’ve also verified that all other mandatory fields in ORDER_ITEMS_IN (such as MATERIAL, TARGET_QTY, TARGET_QU, PLANT) are filled in with valid values.

Despite these efforts, the same error persists.

Has anyone encountered this issue before? Could this be related to how SAP BPA structures the call to the BAPI or expects the table input? 

@BorisAndree Any suggestions or examples of working configurations would be greatly appreciated. Thank you in advance!

SAP Build Process Automation

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

if you have issues with VCM you should implement the notes I have mentioned in this blog.
If this doesn't resolve your issue you should open a support ticket.

Best regards,
Bernd

entry.title: Re: DateTime in OData API response doesn't match with UI

entry.title: Field Values in Intelligent Event Processing custom event
entry.content:
There are two fields ("Event Name" and "Event Context") in the setup of a custom event via Intelligent Event Processing in Cloud ALM. Does anyone have any experience using these two fields?  I hope I can add an object name and then a condition so the event truly is 'Intelligent' in nature. 

I am aware that it is not a good practice to have all custom developed Intelligent Events, but I believe they can be quite useful.

entry.title: Ready to Deploy SAP Fieldglass? There's a Remote Service for That!
entry.content:
SAP Enterprise Support is our foundational support offering - included with your SAP cloud subscription. We deliver "Continuous quality checks" (CQCs) - a large portfolio of remote services designed to reduce technical risks or uncover optimization opportunities. These services cover a wide range of topics, each with a pre-defined scope and checks aligned to SAP best practices. At the end of each CQC, you receive an individualized report with recommendations for improvement.

CQC for Deployment Readiness for SAP Fieldglass

Starting a new project can feel exciting - but also a bit daunting. Why not boost your chances of success by ensuring the quality and stability of your SAP Fieldglass solution right from the start?

  • Want the confidence that your SAP Fieldglass environment has been thoroughly assessed before go-live? There’s a CQC for that!
  • Extending your current SAP Fieldglass suite with additional solutions from SAP?  There’s a CQC for that!
  • Interested in knowing if your SAP Fieldglass solution is functioning optimally?  There’s a CQC for that too!

Whether it’s before the go-live of a new SAP Fieldglass implementation, or when additional solutions are added to the existing SAP Fieldglass suite, or if you need a "health check" for productive SAP Fieldglass systems, the CQC for deployment readiness for SAP Fieldglass is one of many tools we provide that are “included in your cloud subscription.” You will benefit from a detailed analysis of your SAP Fieldglass solution before your go live and best practices for optimal performance and the integration of new solution after go-live.

Ready to Request Remote Services?

Begin by reviewing the CQC for Deployment Readiness for SAP Fieldglass info sheet for guidance.

Access the full list of available CQCs on SAP’s support page: Continuous Quality Check & Improvement Services .

Your Gateway to Realizing ROI

Beyond CQCs, SAP Enterprise Support offers additional resources to help you drive innovation. SAP Enterprise Support value maps provide expert-led resources, tailored recommendations, and advisory services - all included in your SAP cloud subscription. Get started:

  • One-time sign up for SAP Learning Hub, edition for SAP Enterprise Support, using a valid S-User ID (please allow up to 2 hours for processing)
  • Access SAP Enterprise Support value maps for personalized guidance
  • Request a call with our solution area experts for tailored advice

Value Maps are part of SAP Enterprise Support’s commitment to your success. Visit the SAP Enterprise Support value maps landing page to explore our range of topics and get personalized guidance for your SAP Fieldglass journey.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

  About the author:

  • Ernestine Cosper, based in Dallas, Texas, is a Global Topic Owner for SAP Enterprise Support value maps. She’s been with SAP since 2013, holding various roles across the organization. A native of New Orleans, Ernie loves to cook, spend time with her family and dogs, and travel with her husband on his motorcycle - from Dallas all the way to Miami. Fun fact: she’s even been known to nap on the back of the bike! As she puts it:
    “My passion is your success. Moving you forward with the adoption of solutions and helping businesses navigate through SAP Enterprise Support offerings and achieve business transformation.”

Have questions, comments or feedback? Don't hesitate to comment down below or contact @Ernie_Cosper

entry.title: LOA - Termination End Handling
entry.content:
Hi Everyone,

 

Just wanted to confirm if the Termination End Handling | SAP Help Portal was supported for LOA.

 

We are getting the following error: The termination end handling process can’t delimit the employee time because it’s assigned to a leave of absence time type. Please change the dates manually.

 

If not, is there any ways to automatically delimit the end date (Not the actual return date) of the LOA once we terminate and employee?

entry.title: Re: 'Requirement date' flow from 'Work Order' to its EWM 'delivery' planned delivery date
entry.content: We have the same issue in our project. In the Work Order "Requirement Date" is specified for each component but it is not transferred to the Outbound Delivery (VL03N / EWM). How can we ensure that the components are issued by the required date? Now the Planned Good issue Date is taking the Creation Delivery Date, which makes no sense. Anyone can help?

entry.title: Valuation area 5000 is already productive in the material ledgerMessage No. C+338
entry.content:
While trying to delete the plant in OX18 to deactivate Material Ledger (ML), I am encountering an error. Please help me with this issue.

Valuation area 5000 is already productive in the material ledger

Message No. C+338

Diagnosis

You are attempting to change the currency settings and valuation settings for valuation area 5000.  The Material Ledger is already productive for valuation area 5000.

System Response

To guarantee the consitency of your valuation data, this change is not allowed.

entry.title: Unable to brose tags on Plant Connectivity (PCo) UA server
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have created a OPC UA server from a socket source as below

Socket source -> Agent -> OPC UA Server

I have then created a OPC UA source system and am trying to browse tags on the OPC UA Server created as above.

I am expecting to see subscription item of the agent as one of the tags but I do not see it. I see only system tags for PCo there.

Am I missing anything?

entry.title: Re: Advanced Intercompany Sales & Stock Transfer in SAP S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content:
Hello,

1MX - Intercompany Sales Order Processing - International

  • A lean intercompany sales process
  • No intercompany purchase order, no intercompany sales order
  • No valuated stock in transit
  • Available for several sales scenarios

5D2 - Advanced Intercompany Sales

  • A comprehensive intercompany sales process
  • Intercompany purchase order, intercompany sales order
  • Valuated stock in transit (with stock automatic postings based on transfer of control dates)
  • Currently available for sell-from-stock items

1YT - Make-to-Order Production with Variant Configuration

  • Selling a make-to-order product from an intracompany plant

7DM - Advanced Intercompany Sales - Make-to-Order and Variant Config

  • Selling a make-to-order product from an intercompany plant
  • Intercompany purchase order, intercompany sales order
  • Valuated stock in transit (with automatic stock postings based on transfer of control dates)

I'm not aware of any blog where a comparison between the scope items is available.

Best regards,
Bernd

entry.title: SAC - Data Actions - Copy Data between 2 Hierarchies (Parent to leaf)
entry.content:
Dear Gurus,

I have a question regarding copying data in SAC between 2 Hierarchies

I have 2 Hierarchies: 1 at Family level and the second one at articles level for example

I was wondering if it is possible through a data action to copy value from my parent of Hierarchy 1 to the leaf of hierarchy 2 ( Article is the leaf level of the hierarchy 2)

I know with properties it is possible but i was wondering if we could manage the copy by playing with config.hierarchy and a specific syntax in my DATA()=RESULTLOOKUP()

Regards

Julien

entry.title: Re: How to create/show hierarchy in the custom analytical query -S/4HANA Cloud
entry.content: Hello,I'm experiencing the same issue enabling the hierarchy for a custom field. Could you please share how you addressed this?

entry.title: How to request ONB 1.0 Super Admin Access Request for SuccessFactors
entry.content:
Onb Super Admin Access or also known as Onb back-end access

Consultants working on the Onboarding Implementation need this access.  Super Admin site access is required for managing the customer Account.

This request has to be raised only by the consultant certified in C-THR91 or C_THR97 with Partner S user ID via https://hcmcloudops.successfactors.com/

Customers are not allowed access to this portal, even if they try, they will get an Error “ERROR! You have logged in as user Sxxxxxxxxxx belonging to company  XXXXXX  Inc.”

Partner with P id is also not allowed to access this portal, they will get an Error “ERROR! You have logged in as user Pxxxxxxxxxx belonging to partner XXXXXX Inc”

It involves 2 simple steps

Step 1 (Customer Step)

The customer needs to send the below email to the consultant, keeping in mind a few key points

  • Must include the consultant's full name and Partner email address
  • Approval should be in English
  • Customer Approver signature needs to include full name, job position, email, and phone number
  • Attachment must be in a non-editable format, e.g. *.msg/*.eml/*.mht of the approval email.  If your organization does not use MS Outlook, then provide a screenshot(s) of the approval email and attach it to the request. MS Word or other editable documents will not be accepted

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

From:  abcd@qwer.com  (customer)
To: aaaabbbccc@xcde1234.com (consultant)

Subject: SuccessFactors ONB Super Admin Access Approval for <CUSTOMER Name>

I understand SuccessFactors adheres to strict policies concerning access to customer environments. I confirm the approval for CONSULTANT of <PARTNER COMPANY> named below to be provided ONB Super Admin access to my organization’s implementation with details below.

Customer Name: <CUSTOMER Name>
Customer ID: <CUSTOMER ID>
ONB Account Names (aka ONB Company IDs):  <ONB Account Names>
Environment(s):  <e.g., DC2 Production, DC2 Preview>
Consultant Name: Mr./Ms. AAAA  BBB  CCC 
Consultant Email: aaaabbbccc@xcde1234.com

Customer Approver's Signature:

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Step 2 (Consultant Step)

  1. Log in to https://hcmcloudops.successfactors.com/
  2. Go to the Operation Requests tab
  3. Under ONB Requests, click ONB 1.0 Super Admin Access Request
  4. Click ‘Proceed’ on the Certification Required Warning
  5. Provide the mandatory details required by the request form, such as Customer Name, ONB Account, Data Center location, and Environment, and attach the approval email and submit

For finding ONB Account, it will appear as “Account ID” in BizX Provisioning> Company Settings > Onboarding Settings

https://userapps.support.sap.com/sap/support/knowledge/en/2397304

entry.title: Re: Not getting automatic roundoff in FB60
entry.content:
Hello Ganesh,

The tolerance group settings and automatic postings for rounding differences configuration apply during payment processing or open item clearing and not during invoice posting itself (in FB60 or Post Incoming Invoices Fiori app).

Reason: during invoice entry (FB60 or Post Incoming Invoices app), SAP requires the document to balance exactly — line item totals must match the header amount. If they don’t, you’ll get:

"Posting only possible with zero balance."

This is standard system behavior to enforce double-entry integrity, and no tolerance setting can override this at invoice entry time.

Warm regards,

Maciej

entry.title: Re: How to generate a hierarchy based data from flat data...
entry.content: Check Capire ​​https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/advanced/odata#hierarchical-transformations and our sample application https://github.com/SAP-samples/cloud-cap-samples-java where we have a working demo of the hierarchical data.

entry.title: Re: Valuation area 5000 is already productive in the mate...
entry.content: Read (carefully) KBA 3037221 - OX18: Prevent copy/deletion of company code <-> plant assignment if the assigned valuation area has material ledger active

entry.title: Re: Enforce a secure re-authentication for each new SAP GUI connection, with SAP Secure Login Servic
entry.content:
Hi Nelis,

We have the configuration parameter "allowBrowserSSO" that can be used to run the built-in browser of Secure Login Client in a private mode. You could try if this is enough to prevent the Entra Id logon page from picking up any existing token. If it doesn't help then maybe you can define a dedicated policy on Entra Id that always triggers the full authentication.

Best regards,
Christian

entry.title: Re: SAP DM - Roles, Spaces & Groups
entry.content:
Hi @it10 ,

you can't modify the apps that are included in a role template. 

Best,

Mike

entry.title: Re: SAP DM - Roles, Spaces & Groups
entry.content:
Hi @it10 ,

you can't modify the apps included in a role template. 

Best,

Mike

entry.title: Re: Can we load data From Multiple Odata Queries to Single Planning model ?
entry.content: Yes, but. In a account model that is possible but in a measure (new model) that won't be possible until you get release 2025.7. The ability to combine queries was not possible until this release. But with 2025.7 Just like for account models, SAP Analytics Cloud now allows for combining multiple data sources during data preparation of models. This is available either via data import, or when transforming the data foundation data after you have created a model.

entry.title: Re: How can I undeploy a specific field from a table in SAP HANA?
entry.content: Do you keep `managed` on db entity? While you have the definition, field should be present to match it. Projection just models a view on the db entity.

entry.title: Custom data against SFC in SAP DM
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

is there any way to bind custom data against SFC,

use case - we need to maintain the vendor custom number with SFC number so is there any way we can maintain the custom data tags against SFC. 

can anyone please help us that how we can overcome this challenge to in SAP DM.


Regards,
Ankit Gupta

entry.title: Re: Multitenant App is not able to load $metadata of backend service (401 Unauthorized)
entry.content: On the backend side check that the request that triggers 401 has some kind of authentication with it. E.g. JWT token. Also check that your applications connected to the same identity service instances. Your destination configuration can be wrong: `Authentication: NoAuthentication` and `forwardAuthToken: true` are kinda contradictory.

entry.title: Re: Error while Enabling Advance GL Account option - the...
entry.content:
Hi,

Check the GA link in the KBA 3395055. Go to the Migration Issues and check for the topic Migration fails and you will find the explanation you are looking for.

Kr,

Jitin

entry.title: Re: EMIGALL Cook book
entry.content: Try this: https://help.sap.com/doc/910f0b0cd5b740d6b0d8e0b1c97c2e89/1.0/en-US/Data%20Migration%20of%20FI-CA-Based%20Industry%20Solutions.pdf - it worked for me.

entry.title: Auto populate Acct.Assign.Category and settlement receiver in Maintain settlement rule
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

The WBS element will be assigned to the Service Order, Once the Service Order is “Released” we are required to create a Settlement Rule where the WBS will be the cost object. The WBS element which is use in the Service Order will be the receiver for the Settlement Rule. In the Manage Settlement Rule application, after entering the service order and document the account assignment category ( WBS) and settlement receiver ( WBS element from service order header) should be defaulted . Could you please provide solution to default the account assignment category and settlement receiver in Manage Settlement Rule App.

 

Thanks ,

Rajeswari

entry.title: Title: Allocation failed in SAP HANA: Need to allow long-running query to complete without memory er
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I’m encountering the following error when executing a stored procedure in SAP HANA Cloud:

line 79 col 11 (at pos 3216): Allocation failed; Reason: booked memory limit reached (SAP Note 3191224

 

I've already increased:

  • statement_memory_limit

  • global_allocation_limit

But the issue persists.

Context:

  • The error is caused by a single query inside the procedure that performs a complex join and computation.

  • The rest of the procedure runs fine — only this one query consumes excessive memory and causes the procedure to fail.

  • I am not concerned about performance — I just need this query to run successfully even if it takes time.

My Questions:

  1. Is there a way to allow long-running queries to continue even if memory usage is high — without the procedure being terminated?

  2. Are there specific settings, resource group configurations, or query-level coding practices that can:

    • prevent the query from hitting allocation limits, or

    • make it fail gracefully without rolling back the entire transaction?

What I’ve tried:

  • Increased memory-related settings via SQL and HANA Cockpit

  • Refactored some logic (batching, temp tables), but this particular query still crashes

Any guidance on how to make HANA more tolerant of memory-intensive operations or tips to isolate and stabilize heavy queries would be greatly appreciated.

Thanks in advance!

Let me know if you want me to attach code snippets or EXPLAIN PLAN output to this message too.

entry.title: How to know or view who holds Private versions in SAC planning Model
entry.content:
Hello,

One of power user requested that How to know or view who holds Private versions in SAC planning Model.

As developer, I can see users list at Model preference level >Planning > Delete Private versions.

How to provide authorization to user to view the Private versions user list for above property.  I am not looking to view the private versions data to view at Story level.

I shared Model to user so he able to view Model level preferences but unable to view the private versions.  For user the Delete Private versions are grey-out. I didn't see any property at role level to configure Model preferences or Private versions.

Is any other way to view power user that who holds the private versions in the system. 

Regards,

Vasu

entry.title: What's New in 2502 - Release Navigator for Spend Management
entry.content:
Hello Spend Management Community,

We’re excited to let you know that SAP Enterprise Support’s Readiness@Scale team is currently rolling out an update to the Release Navigator for Supply Chain Management, aligned with the upcoming 2505 release.

As part of this update, we’re refreshing and enhancing the related content to reflect the latest improvements. These updates are now underway and will continue through May 2025.

We appreciate your understanding as we transition the content from version 2502 to 2505. Thank you for your continued support—and please see below for more details!

SAP Business Suite Shifts SAP Business Network to Supply Chain Management 

In the first quarter of 2025, SAP unveiled the SAP Business Suite, marking a new era in enterprise management. This suite seamlessly incorporates AI and real-time data into SAP solutions. As businesses face increasing complexities, the next generation of software must be resilient while unlocking new opportunities. Leveraging SAP’s cloud infrastructure, SAP Business Suite provides enhanced flexibility and innovation compared to traditional on-premises systems. Starting with the 2502 Release, the SAP Business Network Release Navigator tile will lead to the Supply Chain Management Release Navigator in the new SAP Business Network section. For a Learn more here

Effective April 2025, SAP Business Network products have been integrated into the Supply Chain Management solution area. Within this area, you can access release information and content related to SAP Business Network in the following areas:

  • Procurement & Supply Chain Collaboration
  • Logistics Collaboration
  • Asset Collaboration

  • SAP Ariba Guided Buying Integration with SAP WalkMe [GUIDEB-1250]

The SAP WalkMe Digital Adoption Platform is now available in Guided buying. Users can take advantage of easy-to-understand guidance powered by SAP WalkMe.

SAP WalkMe is an AI-powered solution that enables teams to create guidance and automation in the flow of work, while giving business and technology leaders insights into how people are using the software and where users can be more self-sufficient. This enables organizations to accelerate user adoption of technology, boost productivity, and maximize the value of enterprise software investments.

The 2505 Release will incorporate release content updates only. To minimize the disruption to customers & partners the Release Navigator for Spend Management will be integrating new UX innovations once per year, in August along with the release content. For each release in February, May & November, only release content will be updated. Here you can find the innovation & content upgrade plans for the end of 2025 & beginning of 2026:

We’d love to hear your thoughts! If you have feedback or ideas for what you'd like to see in future updates of the Release Navigator, we invite you to share them through our feedback survey or by leaving a comment below.

Want to learn more about how the Readiness@Scale team supports release readiness as part of the SAP Enterprise Support portfolio? Check out our Release Ready page. You can also browse all of our blog posts by searching for the Readiness@Scale tag. Thanks for staying engaged and being part of the journey!

Best Run,
Readiness@Scale

SAP Enterprise Support

entry.title: Re: convert nested JSON to ABAP
entry.content:
The question is not specific to JSON, the syntax error is about TYPES only. When used as components, you must NOT refer to Generic types, instead you must use Complete types (complete table category + complete key) see the rules in the ABAP documentation), for instance:

TYPES tty_series TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF ty_single_serie WITH EMPTY KEY.

entry.title: Re: [sap build] application backend failed to load logic canvas
entry.content: yes facing same issue any update on this

entry.content:
Hi @kkenfack 
The blog How to Connect a S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition System to SAP Start  might be helpful:
Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: Can we load data From Multiple Odata Queries to Singl...
entry.content:
HI ,

I got the Solution for this ,

1.Go to Model > Data Management 

2. Select the Import Job  > Set up Import Job 

3.Under the Actions > Combined Data

4. Set the Joining Condition .

 

Regards,

Amarnath

entry.title: 1H 2025 Release Update - Improved Document Migration to OpenText
entry.content:
Migrating documents from SuccessFactors native storage to OpenText is a critical step for customers implementing OpenText. Historically perceived as challenging, this migration process is now being enhanced with focusing on the strategic use of the Check Tool to prepare and monitor the migration. The Check Tool can be used with both solutions SAP SuccessFactors Document Management Core by OpenText and SAP SuccessFactors Extended Enterprise Content Management by OpenText.

The upcoming 1H 2025 release introduces 3 new checks that will help admin users to prepare the system for a smooth document migration, track its progress and effectively manage failures.

The First Step to take - Pre-Migration Check: “OpenText Prerequisites Are Met”

Before initiating the migration, it is imperative to ensure that OpenText prerequisites are met. A common hurdle in migrations is the presence of unsupported characters in file names, such as colons (:) and quotation marks (”), as these characters can cause errors during the transfer to OpenText.

The Check Tool scans the customer’s system for files with these problematic characters and provides a Quick Fix to replace these unsupported characters with an underline (_).

This quick resolution not only saves time but also enhances the overall efficiency of the migration.

Improved Transparency - Status Check: “Determine the Status of Documents Migrated to OpenText”

Once the migration is underway, real-time monitoring of the status of transferred documents is crucial.

The Check Tool now provides information on how many files have been migrated, how many remain, how many failed, and overall progress.

This self-service transparency empowers administrators to track the migration effectively and make informed decisions throughout the process.

Just in Case - Final Check: “Identify attachments that failed to migrate to OpenText”

Despite thorough preparation, some files may still fail to migrate due to unexpected issues like network problems or integration errors. Also here, the Check Tool is equipped to provide support by identifying failed attachments, generating a list of documents that could not be migrated. This identification process is essential for diagnosing and addressing migration failures.

In response to migration failures, the Check Tool offers a Quick Fix option to prepare these attachments for subsequent migration attempts.

Conclusion - Enhanced Admin Experience

Overall, these improvements aim to enhance the migration experience significantly. By offering self-service capabilities, customers can now access migration status and manage failures without needing to engage with SuccessFactors support extensively.

With that said, I hope you agree that the enhanced Check Tool is set to become a pivotal part of the document migration process, bringing new reliability to customers’ OpenText implementations.

If there are any questions open, please feel free to comment on this blog. My colleague (and contributor to this blog) @tarunkumarp and I would be happy to provide answers. Meanwhile, please check out our What’s New Viewer as well as our enhanced documentation.

entry.title: Re: SAP Sapphire Virtual Learning Zone Is Now Live!
entry.content: Thanks for the information, can't wait to see it!!

entry.title: Our customer wants to export entries in entry view, ACDOCA has document splitting,
entry.content:
Hi Team,

Our new Chinese company code has a legal requirement to store all the financial data posted in SAP, maintained in Kingdee system (Chinese government system). When we try to build a report, we face issues with entries in ACDOCA (New universal ledger table in S4HANA). ACDOCA storing entries in the document splitting view.

Businesses want only entries in the entry view (Same as BSEG Table), Is there any way we can actually get entry view line items from ACDOCA table without document splitting line items (Like zero balance line item, Split line item). or is there any other table which we can use as BSEG is no longer used in the future in S4HANA.

We also noticed tax line items are split as per profit centre in ACDOCA and amounts appearing differently in the table which is confusing.

Please let us know how do we solve this issue, Please find the attached document for more details

entry.title: How to copy HDI container with data from production to quality container
entry.content:
Hi,

We implemented a CAP project with HDI container on Hana Cloud tenant.

Solution is in production and growing. First refresh of quality HDI container was done successfully by export/import of HDI container, but now the production is bigger than 2GB so this is not possible anymore.

I've been looking for how to guide blog to refresh quality with production data, but didn't found any alternative.

The best solution looks to setup replication between cloud databases, but it require to recreate many objects and procedure to trigger this.

Is there a simpler way to copy inside the same Hana Cloud tenant a full HDI container to an other ? Export/import is limited and useless when you have onlly one Hana Tenant.

Any hint or feature in this way would be helpfull to ensure good regression testing with current data.

Regards,

Pierre

entry.content:
Hi JuanCarlosDelga,

yes, this PO workflow is standard one which is WS00800238.

for Automatic Release of Purchase Order activity it has step purchase order released.

and it is s/4 hana private cloud edition 2022.

Please let me know if there is any option to achieve this.

Regards

Mohammed

entry.title: Re: SAP CPI: Not able to pick done file
entry.content: Building on Punith's correct answer: The SFTP adapter specifically uses the pattern ${file:name}.done. Here, ${file:name} is a dynamic expression within SAP Integration Suite that automatically resolves to the actual name of the source file being processed. So, if the adapter picks up abc.TXT, this expression evaluates to abc.TXT.done. The adapter waits for this exact .done file as confirmation that abc.TXT has been completely written to the server. This prevents the integration flow from reading an incomplete file during transfer. Regards, F.

entry.title: 1H 2025 Release Update - Enhancements in the Area of Document Management
entry.content:
In the new 1H 2025 release, administrators will find some interesting and helpful improvements around Manage Document Services.

One focus of the upcoming release was to enhance our Manage Documents page to provide an intuitive and well-structured user interface, aiming to simplify interaction and optimize the structure of information.

Let's take a closer look at these significant updates and how they enhance the user experience.

The Manage Documents  page has been redesigned to offer clearer access to various information with three central tabs: "Documents," "Document Categories," and "Document Storage." This structure helps users quickly find and organize the information they need.

What’s in the Documents Tab?

The "Documents" tab is centered around providing a comprehensive overview of all available documents. It has been enhanced to include additional metadata and filtering options, allowing administrators to access document details swiftly:

  • By default, Document Entity and Document Category are now included as list columns. Other metadata are available too and can easily be added to the list, using “View Settings”.
  • The "Uploaded By" information now combines the user’s first and last names, providing a more natural appearance . If the user prefers to have each information in a single column of the list – “View Settings” will make that possible too.
  • Additionally, we introduced a new Advanced Filters option that allows administrators to list all documents that are not associated with any document entity type and document category. These documents were previously categorized under “Other” in Document Storage but couldn’t be identified in the result table. 

Hello Document Categories Tab!

Previously lurking in the shadows, managing document categories have now emerged into the limelight with their very own tab. Administrators can now easily view and manage existing document categories as well as create new ones:

  • A new, intuitive dialog allows users to create custom document categories. Once saved, these custom categories appear at the top of the list, instead of its end, enhancing clarity. 

  • The new feature that allows sorting the list by Document Entity or Category Type empowers administrators to focus on the information they are currently most interested in, whether that means viewing all custom document categories at a glance or listing all categories created for a specific entity type, regardless of whether they are predefined or custom created.

Everything’s Here at Last: Document Storage Tab. 

Ever felt like playing detective just to figure out what’s eating up your document storage? Those days are over! The Document Storage tab finally provides a complete overview of all categories contributing to storage consumption:

  • By removing the elusive "Others" category, showing all existing document categories, and adding the new “Uncategorized ” category exclusively for documents not tied to any entity type and document category, administrators now get full visibility into how each category contributes to overall storage consumption. 
  • The previous "View More Information" feature has been thoughtfully removed to streamline the user experience. By focusing on reliable, essential data, this update decreases confusion and enhances overall clarity, ensuring that users access the most meaningful information without distraction.

I hope you’ll agree that these enhancements to the Manage Documents page elevate the document management admin experience. We’ve taken significant strides toward improving efficiency and transparency in the handling of documents for admin users.

If there are any questions open, please feel free to comment on this blog. I’d be happy to provide answers. Meanwhile, please check out our What’s New Viewer as well as our enhanced documentation.

entry.title: Re: Queries on CLT: Myanmar - GL settings
entry.content:
Hi Duarte 

On Question number 1

You can not define your own accounting principle. Please check help document link Assign Accounting Principles to Ledgers and Company Codes | SAP Help Portal

But you can assign any accounting principles that are in scope in the corresponding system.

Regards

Rahul

entry.title: sap ibp net requirement - simple calculation without using S&OP heuristic or copy operator
entry.content:
Hi,

I am trying to perform the type of calculation described in the question below

basically based on Initial Inventory, Demand, Safety Stock Requirement, Days of Supply Requirement, I want to calculate my replenishment plan and projected inventory...

This is a recursive calculation, as to determine the Replenishment of M, I need Inventory of M-1 and to determine Inventory of M I need replenishment of M and inventory M-1

So I get into the loop trap as well explained in the question below as well

Cycle found in the calculation chain of the key fi... - SAP Community

But there was no final answer to this... any one would have some solution?

see also attached example from excel

Many thanks for your help !

Jean-Luc

entry.title: Re: BRF+ in SAP MDG: Unable to get BP Central Data fields...
entry.content:
I have already added BP_CENTRL using the "Add Existing Data Object" option in the Signature tab, and I can see the central data fields listed there. 

I'm encountering the issue after creating the RuleSet and navigating to the object within the Assigned RuleSet. The problem occurs when I try to add a validation condition by inserting a rule — the expected Central Data fields are not accessible.

Is there an additional configuration step I'm missing, or something else I need to do to make these fields accessible in the Ruleset context?

Any guidance would be appreciated.

entry.title: Learning - Manager Overdue notifications
entry.content:
With the 1H2025 release, SAP is allowing managers to edit the overdue notifications. While this is generally a good thing, there are concerns about managers removing too much information from the email template (mainly what's overdue and due dates). We would like for SAP to split the permissions for the sending and editing of the notifications so that companies can choose if they want managers to be able to edit or not. I have submitted an enhancement request (link below). If you would please take a moment and go vote for the request so that companies that would have the choice based on the permissions in the role.

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/346459

entry.title: Re: Treating Business Goals as "Individual" Goals possible?
entry.content:
Hi @mkrieg 

Business goals are not designed to have different results by employee, only by a specific attribute (location, business unit, country....). Once you have that attribute then you can do what you are describing and have different results by attribute using Interpolate or Step functions. 

The only way to build what you are describing would be to load one business goal in the system for each employee which is worst practice in terms of design and impacts job processing times (opening executive review, updating worksheets, save). 

Try keeping these goals where result differ by employee in the Goal Management so they can feed the performance form and then VarPay. If they need to have a specific weighting that's different from other individual goals then maybe place them in their own category in GM so they can feed a separate section of the performance form and so you can retrieve only those in the Ind Section or Team Section of the Varpay worksheet with the right weight applied.

Yes knowing Variable Pay well actually means knowing PM-GM....

All the best

Xavier

entry.title: Re: Blog it Forward - Raquel Cunha - version 2025
entry.content: @Lluis ☺️ I’m happy to know I helped you. Thank you for your kind words.

entry.title: Smart Multi Edit for doesn't load fields
entry.content:
Hi experts, 
I need to create mass edit functionality for my child entity. The problem is, this entity is defined as a view with joins on other entities, so it is impossible for me to implement this via just setting 

"tableSettings": {									 
   "enableMassEdit": true
}

in manifest.json for the table: I get error "UPDATE on views with join and/or union is not supported"

So I decided to try using Smart Multi Edit. I use class sap.fe.macros.Table in a XML fragment for my table, and it is the only difference from the sample from documentation, where Smart Table is used

https://ui5.sap.com//#/entity/sap.ui.comp.smartmultiedit.Container/sample/sap.ui.comp.sample.smartmultieditfriendly/code

While debugging I see, that method

container.setContexts(myTable.getSelectedContexts());
sets selected rows to the container, and the pop up from my fragment is shown, but doesn't contain the field that I defined

When I output console.log(container.getEntitySet());

I get ParentEntity/childNavigationField, not ChildEntity

Can it be the reason? Is the problem in using not Smart Table?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Business Partner Conversion - Merge Customer and Vendor into single Business Partner
entry.content:
Dear Andi,

Thank you very much for your highly valuable contribution.

In the SAP ERP system, the transactions FLBPD1, FLBPD2, FLBPC1, and FLBPC2 are used to create business partners from customer or vendor master data and to establish the corresponding links.

In SAP S/4HANA, I manually created a business partner using transaction BP and assigned the Customer role.
Now, I would like to link this same business partner to a Vendor that belongs to the same legal or economic entity.

Currently, I am trying to add the Vendor (FLVN00) role to the existing business partner, but I receive an error due to an incompatible account group (You cannot create a vendor with groupping KOST).

My question is:
Do I need to define a vendor account group in Customizing that mirrors the existing customer account group KOST, using the same name and number range? (SAP Customizing Implementation Guide -> Cross-Application Components -> Master Data Synchronization -> Customer/Vendor Integration -> Business Partner Settings -> Settings for Vendor Integration -> Define BP Role for Direction Vendor to BP)


Or what is the correct procedure to follow in this case?

 

Thank you in advance.

Kind regards,
Emal Rahman

entry.title: Re: what is the role of functional consultant in SAP BTP
entry.content:
As a Functional Consultant, your role in SAP BTP is to understand what BTP has to offer and design smarter processes using tools like SAP Build, Work Zone, and SAC (without writing any code).

For SAP EAM, you could create a maintenance request approval workflow using SAP Build Process Automation  with no coding requirement, just configuration and logic.

I’ve written a full guide on this transition from ECC/S4 to BTP 
SAP BTP FUNCTIONAL CONSULTANT 

Hope it helps!

entry.title: Re: Excel Pop up issue in some reports
entry.content: Nice post thanks Anil

entry.title: Re: Analytical queries in BTP ABAP
entry.content:
Dear Rammel,

In general, the InA UI (Intelligent Data Access) connection can also be used for integration with other SAP applications and services.
For example, it can also be used in conjunction with other SAP tools and platforms for analytical purposes, allowing users to access data in different SAP
environments.
With regard to SAP HANA, the Information Access (InA) infrastructure enables, for example, the implementation of applicationsfor analyzing, planning,
and searching data stored in SAP HANA.
The InA model enables the definition of queries with comprehensive or complex semantics. The query can read data from all types of SAP HANA views
and EPM planning data containers. The InA model also includes spatial functions and search functions.

Check here: Information Access (InA) Queries | SAP Help Portal

 

Best regards

Dennis

entry.title: Re: Create Sales Order in S4/HANA from SAP IBP
entry.content: Thank you Joy, can I use an ODATA API URL as a REST datstore in CI DS?

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA - Extensibility Inventory fiori App
entry.content: Thanks for sharing. Could you show an example of CDS views with an obsolete task category?

entry.title: Re: /UI2/CL_DATA_ACCESS and JSONPATH
entry.content:
Dear SuhasGavas,

In the ABAP Cloud, the class /UI2/CL_JSON could be used to parse JSON instead of the class /UI2/CL_DATA_ACCESS.

See examples here: 

ABAP Cloud - JSON Conversion

ABAP Code Samples : ABAP and JSON – Discovering ABAP

Best regards

Dennis

entry.title: Re: Using BTP Abode form service in UI5 custom app
entry.content:
Dear Vikasyadav,

Unfortunately, the "500 Internal Server Error" error is too general and doesn't provide a specific error description. You should check the connection from BAS (Business Application Studio) to the target system step by step.


Can you successfully call other services from BAS?


Here are some troubleshooting steps that can help you analyze the problem:


Destination Configuration
1) Ensure that the Destination configuration in BAS is correct.

2) Check the URL, authentication method, and other parameters.

3) Use the test tool in the target to ensure that the connection to the service URL is successful.


Authentication and Permissions

1) Ensure that the user credentials used in your UI5 application have the required permissions. These may be different from the
credentials used in Postman. Compare the request headers and payload.
2) Compare the headers and payload of the request sent by your UI5 application with those in Postman. Pay attention to differences in the
headers (e.g., content-type, authorization).
3) Ensure the payload structure is identical, especially for POST requests. Monitor network traffic in debug mode.
4) Use your browser's developer tools (F12) to monitor network traffic. Review the request and response for more information about the
error.


Backend logs
If possible, check the backend logs to see if any errors or exceptions were logged while processing your request.
I hope these steps will help you get closer to solving your problem.

Best regards

Dennis

entry.title: Re: how to read dynamic attribute value
entry.content:
Dear Mark_Fryu,

To read the value of an attribute from an XML file, you can use an XPath query. Since the attribute doesn't have a fixed value, you'll use a general query
to extract the attribute instead of a specific value.
Let's assume you've created a WebDriver object and are on the page containing the XML document, or have already loaded it.
Reading the attribute might then look like this in Java code (Java is used as an example here, and depending on your preferred language, you can of
course use a different language)


…
// Create the object
WebDriver myDriver = new ChromeDriver();
// Load the XML-File
myDriver.get("Filepath");
// Read the attribute
String attributeVal = myDriver.findElement(By.xpath("//tes2")).getAttribute("ns1:testattribute");

...

Best regards

Dennis

entry.title: How to merge a Business Partner to Both Customer and Vendor Roles in SAP S/4HANA?
entry.content:
Dear SAP S/4HANA Experts,

In the SAP ERP system, transactions FLBPD1, FLBPD2, FLBPC1, and FLBPC2 are used to create business partners from customer or vendor master data and to establish the appropriate links.

In SAP S/4HANA, I manually created a business partner using transaction BP and assigned the Customer role. I would now like to associate this same business partner with a Vendor that belongs to the same legal or economic entity.

At present, I am attempting to add the Vendor role (FLVN00) to the existing business partner. However, I encounter an error message indicating an incompatible account group:
“You cannot create a vendor with grouping KOST.”

My question is as follows:
Is it necessary to define a vendor account group in Customizing that mirrors the existing customer account group KOST—using the same name and number range—and to link this vendor account group to the business partner role FLVN00? (Path: SAP Customizing Implementation Guide → Cross-Application Components → Master Data Synchronization → Customer/Vendor Integration → Business Partner Settings → Settings for Vendor Integration → Define BP Role for Direction Vendor to BP)

Or is there a different recommended approach to achieve this integration correctly?

Kind regards,
Emal Rahman

entry.title: Re: Adobe Form/Smartform with RAP-Based OData Service Without SEGW
entry.content:
Nice blog thanks for sharing @UsamaKhan 

Thanks-

entry.title: Certficate expiry notification in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We have received notification regarding the certificate expiry for Baltimore CyberTrust Root Certificate on 13th May 2025 in Integration Suite. We are not sure whether it really affects us,  There are certain steps that needs to be taken mentioned in https://me.sap.com/notes/3565626/E  . Eventhough this certificate is not used currently, it is better to update the certificate right? Pls guide regarding this.

 

 

Thanks,

Ramu.

entry.title: Re: Certficate expiry notification in SAP Integration Suite
entry.content:
Hi Ramu, Here's the bottom line based on SAP Note 3565626:

  • What's happening? This specific root certificate expires May 2025. Any client certificates signed by it will stop working.
  • Are you affected? You are potentially affected only if systems calling into Integration Suite use a client certificate signed by Baltimore CyberTrust Root. You might also be affected if your Integration Suite flows calling out to other systems are configured in their receiver adapters (e.g., HTTP/OData) to use a specific private key alias for client certificate authentication, and that alias corresponds to a certificate signed by Baltimore CyberTrust Root.
  • What to do - Check: Verify the CAs signing any client certificates used for inbound authentication. Also, check your iFlow receiver adapter configurations for any specific key aliases used for outbound authentication and verify the CA of the corresponding certificates.
  • What to do - Act if needed: If your checks reveal any dependencies on the Baltimore root, you must replace the affected certificates or update configurations before May 2025. This might involve getting new certificates signed by a different supported CA or switching outbound flows to use the standard sap_cloudintegrationcertificate if appropriate.
  • What to do - If no impact: If your checks confirm none of your scenarios use client certificates tied to this Baltimore root, you don't need to do anything regarding this specific alert.
  • Ignore the root in Keystore: Don't worry about the Baltimore CyberTrust Root certificate itself shown in the Integration Suite Keystore; its presence doesn't require action.

In short: Check authentication methods for inbound calls and specific key aliases used in outbound adapter configurations within your iFlows. Replace certificates or update settings only if they rely on the expiring Baltimore root.

Hope this simplifies it!

F


Cloud Integration
